title: Closed-form recurrence bridge
summary: Records the conditional bridge from a supplied rational defect-cancellation proof to the closed-form recurrence predicate and imports the independently proved two local Pochhammer recurrences.

mathematicalContent.heading: Conditional global statement
mathematicalContent.body: If the regular coefficient system already satisfies every rational defect-cancellation equation for a chosen basis, then the regular closed formula satisfies the recurrence predicate—because that predicate is definitionally the same condition.

mathematicalContent.heading: Unconditional local statement
mathematicalContent.body: The two Lemma 3.1 adjacent-index identities for the Pochhammer factor have been proved in RatioLemmas and are re-exported here.

correspondence.leanType: theorem ORRegularClosedFormula_satisfiesRecurrence_of_defectCancellationQ {N : ℕ} {κ : Type _} [DecidableEq κ] (n w₁ w₂ : ℚ) (K : Finset κ) (defectCoeffQ : ORIndex N → Fin 2 → κ → ℚ) (hcancel : SatisfiesDefectCancellationQ K (ORCoefficientSystem.regular N n w₁ w₂) defectCoeffQ) : ORRegularClosedFormula.SatisfiesRecurrence n w₁ w₂ K defectCoeffQ
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Shows that a supplied rational cancellation proof is exactly enough to inhabit the closed formula's recurrence predicate.

correspondence.leanType: theorem ORRegularClosedFormula_satisfiesLemma31LocalRecurrences {N : ℕ} (n w₁ w₂ : ℚ) : (∀ I : ORIndex N, ORRegularAFirstRecurrence n w₁ w₂ I) ∧ (∀ I : ORIndex N, ORRegularASecondRecurrence n w₁ w₂ I)
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: States the already proved pair of local Lemma 3.1 recurrences for the regular Pochhammer coefficients.

references.locator: Classification of invariant bilinear differential operators
references.note: Flat-model origin of the coefficient family.
Primary curved ambient source for the encoded OR recurrence.

limitations: The first theorem assumes the full rational defect cancellation and then returns it through a definitional wrapper; it does not derive global cancellation from the local recurrences. Only the two local coefficient identities are unconditional.
